question.text: SEBI regulations require commodity exchanges to have:

answer.text: Proper governance structure, adequate net worth, robust technology, risk management systems, and investor protection mechanisms
Only a website and a trading platform
Only SEBI registration — no other requirements
Physical trading floors in every state

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI requires exchanges to have: proper governance (board composition, committees), adequate net worth, robust technology infrastructure, comprehensive risk management, investor protection, and surveillance capabilities. **The Trap:** Requirements are comprehensive, not just a website. Registration alone is insufficient. No physical floor requirement. **Cross-Exam Context:** Exchange governance standards are set by SEBI. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Exchange governance includes mandatory independent directors, investor protection committees, and regular SEBI inspections — these safeguards protect all market participants.
